seo.title: Building Code Generation with Azure AI Agent Service + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build code generation with Azure AI Agent Service + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.
keywords: Azure AI Agent Service code generation

content.intro: Azure AI Agent Service enables powerful code generation agents powered by Azure OpenAI, with built-in grounding and enterprise security. However, generating executable code introduces high risks like credential leakage, unsafe dependencies, and policy violations. Vex provides essential guardrails to validate code safety, syntax, and execution before deployment.

architectureOverview.heading: Code Generation Architecture with Azure AI Agent Service
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture starts with Azure AI Agent Service orchestrating an agent thread that uses Azure OpenAI models for code generation. User prompts trigger the agent to generate code, which is grounded with Bing Search and file retrieval from Azure storage. The generated code is executed in a sandboxed environment with test validation, followed by iterative refinement based on execution results and feedback.

Key components include the Agent Thread API for conversation management, tool calling for sandbox execution, and Azure-specific grounding data sources. Vex integrates as middleware to intercept agent responses, validate generated code against enterprise policies, and ensure safe execution before proceeding.

This setup supports iterative refinement loops where failed validations trigger the agent to regenerate safer code.

risks.name: Tool execution results not validated against enterprise policies
risks.description: Generated code may execute tools or access resources violating corporate security policies
risks.mitigation: Vex policy scanner intercepts and validates all tool calls and execution results

risks.name: Grounding data from Bing Search containing outdated information
risks.description: Agents may generate code based on stale API documentation or deprecated libraries
risks.mitigation: Vex freshness validator checks grounding data recency before code generation

risks.name: File search returning Azure-specific metadata instead of content
risks.description: Agents extract Azure resource metadata instead of actual code/content from files
risks.mitigation: Vex content extractor strips metadata and validates file content relevance

risks.name: Agent thread token limits exceeded without graceful degradation
risks.description: Long code generation threads hit token limits, losing context and producing broken code
risks.mitigation: Vex token monitor summarizes conversations and enforces graceful thread truncation

implementation.title: Initialize Azure AI Agent Client with Vex Middleware
implementation.description: Create agent thread client with Vex integrated as response validator for code generation workflows
implementation.code:
from azure.ai.projects import AgentClient
from vex_sdk import VexGuardrail

# Initialize Azure AI Agent Client
agent_client = AgentClient(
    endpoint="https://your-project.openai.azure.com/",
    api_key="your-api-key"
)

# Vex middleware for code generation
guardrail = VexGuardrail(
    policies=["code_safety", "no_credentials", "syntax_valid"],
    use_case="code_generation"
)

thread = agent_client.create_thread()

print(f"Agent thread ready with Vex guardrails: {thread.id}")

implementation.title: Code Generation with Sandbox Execution
implementation.description: Agent generates Python code and executes in Docker sandbox with Vex validation
implementation.code:
async def generate_and_execute_code(agent_client: AgentClient, thread_id: str, prompt: str):
    from vex_sdk import CodeValidator
    
    # Send code generation prompt
    response = await agent_client.send_message(
        thread_id=thread_id,
        content=prompt,
        tools=[{"type": "code_interpreter"}]
    )
    
    # Extract generated code
    generated_code = response.content[0].text
    
    # Vex validates before execution
    validator = CodeValidator()
    if validator.is_safe(generated_code):
        result = await agent_client.run_tool(
            thread_id=thread_id,
            tool="sandbox_execute",
            code=generated_code
        )
        return result
    else:
        raise ValueError("Code failed Vex validation")
